Title: RNAi-mediated endogene silencing in strawberry fruit: detection of primary and secondary siRNAs by deep sequencing

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase, chalcone/stilbene synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with three malonyl-CoA molecules to form naringenin chalcone, an entry metabolite to flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: essential pathway enzyme; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi-mediated down-regulation of FaCHS reduces anthocyanin accumulation, especially pelargonidin 3-O-glucoside, causing loss of red pigmentation/chimeric white sectors in strawberry fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. All five overlapping ihp constructs spanning FaCHS significantly reduced FaCHS transcript abundance in strawberry fruit, generally by >80%. 2. FaCHS silencing caused visible loss of red pigmentation and reduced pelargonidin 3-O-glucoside and related pelargonidin derivatives. 3. Transitive silencing targeting FaCHS via GUS-CHS fusion also reduced pelargonidin derivatives but was less effective than direct ihp silencing.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration of ripening fruit 14 days after pollination; fruit analyzed at ripe stage under standard growth conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient RNAi agroinfiltration with inverted hairpin constructs; qPCR; fruit phenotype observation; LC-MS metabolite profiling; transitive silencing assay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: small RNA-seq on Illumina HiSeq 2000 for study context; no FaCHS-specific accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced red fruit coloration due to lowered anthocyanin content.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful functional target for engineering strawberry fruit color and flavonoid composition; direct silencing/editing would be expected to decrease anthocyanin pigmentation, so it is more suitable as a marker or for pathway studies than for nutritional enhancement.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FaCHS was a core endogenous target used to compare direct ihp silencing with transitive silencing. Direct ihp constructs were more effective than transitive vectors.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates HDMF to form DMMF in strawberry fruit; the paper also notes FaOMT can form feruloyl glucose relative to caffeoyl glucose in prior work.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for DMMF formation in the described step; not explicitly called rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi-mediated down-regulation of FaOMT markedly decreases the methylated furanone DMMF and increases the proportion of its substrate HDMF among total furanones, without an obvious visible fruit phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. All five overlapping ihp constructs spanning FaOMT significantly reduced FaOMT transcript levels, generally by >80%. 2. FaOMT silencing reduced DMMF formation and shifted fruit furanone composition toward HDMF. 3. Deep sequencing after FaOMT RNAi detected abundant primary siRNAs and low-abundance secondary siRNAs outside the target region, providing evidence for transitive silencing spreading mainly in the 3′ direction. 4. Primary siRNAs exceeded secondary siRNAs by about three orders of magnitude, explaining why direct ihp silencing was more effective than transitive silencing.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration of ripening fruit 14 days after pollination; small RNA sequencing after pBI-FaOMT_Ci and pBI-FaOMTi treatment; standard growth conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient RNAi agroinfiltration with inverted hairpin constructs; qPCR; LC-MS metabolite profiling; small RNA deep sequencing; read mapping to FaOMT
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: small RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq 2000); 14,288,304 reads for OMT_short and 21,722,563 reads for OMT_long; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced methylated flavor volatile DMMF; no visible phenotype reported. Feruloyl glucose response was masked by agroinfiltration-induced phenylpropanoid changes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable target for modifying strawberry flavor composition by tuning HDMF-to-DMMF conversion; direct editing or expression modulation could alter aroma quality, but effects on other phenylpropanoid-related products and treatment-induced metabolic background should be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FaOMT was the main gene used to demonstrate secondary siRNA formation in strawberry fruit. Secondary siRNAs were detected upstream and especially downstream of the targeted region, indicating spreading of RNA silencing.
